expectations.text: Pre-eclampsia risk classified as HIGH (nulliparity + age >35 + BMI >30 + chronic hypertension)
expectations.evidence: The note does not use formal risk classification terminology (HIGH/MODERATE/LOW). It mentions 'higher risk for complications' vaguely under advanced maternal age and says 'Consider low-dose aspirin for pre-eclampsia prevention given risk factors (advanced age, first pregnancy, chronic hypertension, obesity)' but never formally classifies the pre-eclampsia risk as HIGH. The risk factors are listed loosely but not systematically categorized.

expectations.text: Aspirin 75mg daily recommended starting from 12 weeks
expectations.evidence: The note says 'Consider low-dose aspirin for pre-eclampsia prevention given risk factors' but does not specify the dose (75mg), does not mention the 12-week start time, does not specify daily frequency, and uses 'consider' rather than a firm recommendation. No stop date (36 weeks) is mentioned either.
Calcium supplementation is not mentioned anywhere in the note. Only 'prenatal vitamins including iron and folic acid' are mentioned under medications.

The note says 'Regular blood pressure checks at each visit' and 'Return in 4-6 weeks for next antenatal visit' but does not explicitly increase monitoring frequency beyond standard ANC. There is no mention of more frequent contacts or compressed schedule due to high-risk status. A 4-6 week return interval is essentially standard, not intensified.
The Monitoring section states 'Monitor for signs of pre-eclampsia (proteinuria, headache, visual changes, swelling)' which does include proteinuria monitoring as part of pre-eclampsia surveillance.

expectations.text: Current BP (135/88) flagged as elevated but not in immediate danger range
expectations.evidence: The note describes BP as 'mildly elevated' (135/88 mmHg) and states 'No current signs of pre-eclampsia' which implicitly indicates it is not in the immediate danger range. However, the characterization is less precise than the with-skill version -- it does not explicitly state it is not >=160/110.
